design_philosophy.core_loop: Build dungeon infrastructure → recruit and train minions → defend against hero invasions and rival keepers → expand territory and technology tree
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (gold/gems) vs. creature morale vs. incoming hero threats; balancing offense against defense
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as a dark lord commanding evil forces; manage a living dungeon ecosystem with personality and consequence
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Inverts traditional fantasy narrative by casting player as the villain; emphasizes dark humor and emergent creature behavior over scripted storytelling; creatures have agency (can vandalize, desert, demand wages) creating systemic challenge beyond combat

difficulty.lives_system: Dungeon Heart serves as single life point; destruction of Heart = game loss
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through infrastructure design choices, creature recruitment strategy, and room/trap placement; player must manage creature morale and resource economy to survive escalating hero waves

progression.upgrade_system: Technology tree unlocking rooms and spells as game progresses; creature experience/training system increases individual minion attributes and spell access
progression.unlock_mechanism: Linear progression through dungeon heart advancement; certain creatures require prerequisite rooms to enter service; sacrifices at Temple unlock additional minions
replayability_hooks: Multiplayer support (up to 4 players via modem/LAN)
Multiple minion recruitment paths (portal arrivals vs. torture vs. scavenging vs. sacrifice)
Creature ecosystem with natural enemy relationships requiring strategic housing
Final boss (Avatar) encountered at end requiring specific preparation

world_building_techniques: Sinister voiceover mentor providing situational guidance
Color-coded faction system (player=red, rivals=other colors, heroes=white) establishing visual world hierarchy
Creature ecology with natural enemy relationships embedded in game systems
Dark humor in mechanics (slapping creatures damages them, sacrificing to gods for rewards/punishments)
Portal arrivals and creature recruitment reflect dungeon progression state

boss_design.philosophy: Avatar serves as final confrontation representing heroic opposition; encounter design likely scales with player's dungeon development and creature roster
notable_bosses: Avatar (final hero, resembles Ultima VIII: Pagan Avatar)

faction_indicators: Keeper color identity (red for player, distinct colors for AI rivals)
Creature recruitment reflects dungeon values (dragons/warlocks vs. undead)
Temple sacrifices reveal player's relationship with dark powers
Rival keeper presence in each realm creates competing dungeon kingdoms
danger_signals: Hero invasions escalate as dungeon grows
Creature morale system (angry minions vandalize dungeon and desert)
Payday wages create economic pressure requiring gold management
Natural enemy creature pairs create internal dungeon conflicts
environmental_storytelling: Dungeon layout reflects keeper's strategic priorities (fortress-like vs. trap-heavy)
Creature housing choices indicate faction alignment
Treasury size and security suggest economic health
Temple frequency reveals engagement with dark forces
Trap density around entrances indicates defensive philosophy
